short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $2.27B in FY2026 Q1, +6.1% YoY — stable trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $3.50B (+54.2% vs current Q) — set by 38 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $1.85.
Margin context: 6.8% net margin (1.7% op margin) — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-08-06 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $10B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $12B → FY2 $14B (+12.3% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 14.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
7-quarter run-rate CAGR: -5.8% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Consumer Discretionary): Premiumization vs trade-down split — the long-run thesis depends on cohort mix.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 39.1% across 8 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 8 dates spanning 90–608 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±19.2% ($106.99 – $157.71).
390-day implied move: ±41.0% ($78.11 – $186.59) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $155 (+17.1% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Flat term structure (+3.6pp) — no near-term event premium; long-dated vol fairly priced.
long_term.strategy: Buy 1-year LEAPS call at $119 (10% ITM, 70-delta proxy). Long-cycle trend up + sell-side sees +17.1% upside to $155 PT → ITM LEAPS gives ~80% of stock exposure at ~40% of capital, with limited downside vs holding.
